title: Easton police looking for dirt bikers who caused ‘thousands’ in damage at country club
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Easton police search for two dirt bikers causing damage at Easton Country Club, costing thousands.
Surveillance images captured the riders trespassing the club on Purchase Street, last seen near Prospect Street tracks.
Public urged to contact Officer Tyler Sutton with information on the bikers or their dirt bikes at 508-230-3322.

title: This Dorchester resident brings trash to City Hall to raise awareness of the litter problem in Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Dorchester resident Alex Alex collects litter and damaged infrastructure, leaving it at City Hall to highlight Boston's waste management issues.
Alex has gathered over 5,000 pounds of trash in a year, using social media to raise awareness of his efforts.
City Councilors claim they are responsive to Alex's concerns, while he plans further protests and community cleanups.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White shares beer with Jimmy Fallon, talks dental work on ‘The Tonight Show’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics guard Derrick White appeared on 'The Tonight Show' to discuss basketball, fatherhood, and dental injuries.
White introduced his new collaboration beer, Derrick White Ale, as part of Sam Adams' 'Our City. Our Beer' collection.
White also recounted losing a tooth during a game after a collision with 76ers forward Kelly Oubre Jr. in March 2025.

title: Nashua, N.H. man arrested after allegedly assaulting 3-month-old son
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Drew Babcock, 36, of Nashua was arrested for allegedly assaulting his 3-month-old son, causing serious injuries.
The child reportedly suffered fractured ribs, femurs, and clavicle, prompting police investigation after state notification.
Babcock faces eight counts of first degree domestic assault, pleading not guilty and remains in custody before bail hearing.

title: Here’s how much a remote per diem worker makes north of Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Francine M., a 66-year-old remote worker from Hamilton-Wenham, earns $27,000 annually working per diem as a senior project manager.
She transitioned to remote work during COVID, moving from full-time to per diem in 2023 with a focus on clinical research.
Francine supplements her income with a pension and 403(b), noting her biggest expense is healthcare since switching to Medicare.

title: NTSB: Flight instructor in fatal Mass. plane crash had ‘do not fly’ meds in system
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three people died in a 2024 plane crash near Leyden, Massachusetts, including instructor William Hampton with 'do not fly' meds in his system.
NTSB found CNS depressants like alprazolam, amitriptyline, and zolpidem in Hampton, affecting his capacity to manage the plane.
FAA had previously revoked Hampton's medical certificate due to DUIs and medication use but later reinstated it after claims of discontinuing meds.

title: WATCH: Bagpiper performs on St. Patrick’s Day in Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Bagpiper Nate Silva performed at Harry's Bar and Grill in Boston on St. Patrick's Day.
Silva played for over 30 locations in four days, marking one of his busiest periods annually.
Nate Silva, known as @passionpiping, has been playing since age 11, performing at 100 events annually.

title: 50 ways to make the most of spring in Massachusetts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth closed after over 20 years, with a new restaurant opening at the location in April.
Boston hotels and Airbnb hosts are increasing prices for summer's FIFA World Cup, with Airbnb offering a $750 bonus for new hosts.
JetBlue canceled its Boston-Asheville route planned for May, offering refunds to affected customers due to poor performance.

title: Tom Brady brought jokes, nostalgia, and a comeback tease to Jimmy Fallon ahead of flag football debut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tom Brady teased his return to the field for the Fanatics Flag Football Classic during an appearance on Jimmy Fallon's show.
Brady, now with a broadcasting role at FOX, discussed his changed perspective on football post-retirement.
A flag football event, featuring Brady and Rob Gronkowski, was relocated to Los Angeles due to conflicts in the Middle East.

title: This is the current state of the TSA at Logan Airport
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: TSA agents at Logan Airport continue working without pay amid a government shutdown, despite a few resignations.
Logan has maintained normal security lines as Massport reports no staffing issues, contrasting other airports facing delays.
Massport tests new camera system for real-time TSA wait times, unrelated to the government shutdown woes.
